top_stories.title: Announcing The Forrester Wave™: Workforce Identity Platforms, Q2 2026
top_stories.summary: Forrester has released its Q2 2026 Wave evaluation covering workforce identity security platforms. The report assesses and ranks major IAM vendors competing in the workforce identity space, serving as a key reference for enterprise purchasing decisions.

top_stories.title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
top_stories.summary: New Omdia research examines how AI agent identity management is creating distinct budget dynamics compared to traditional IAM projects. The research focuses on how enterprises must manage, secure, and govern the proliferating identities of AI agents across the organization.

top_stories.title: Deleted Google API keys keep working for up to 23 minutes, researchers warn
top_stories.summary: Aikido Security researchers discovered that deleted Google API keys remain functional for up to 23 minutes after deletion due to caching delays in Google's authentication infrastructure. This affects credential revocation reliability, meaning leaked API keys that are deleted may still be exploited within that window to authenticate, make API calls, and potentially access Gemini AI files and cached conversations.

top_stories.title: Versa extends zero trust principles to AI agents and MCP workflows
top_stories.summary: Versa has introduced a patent-pending zero trust architecture for the Model Context Protocol (MCP), applying identity-based validation to AI agent actions in enterprise environments. Every AI-generated action is checked against user identity, role-based access controls, and system policies before execution, with optional human approval gates configurable by administrators.

top_stories.title: When Identity is the Attack Path
top_stories.summary: An analysis examines how a single cached AWS access key on a Windows machine can expose up to 98% of a company's cloud entities to attackers. The piece illustrates how standard credential caching behavior, rather than misconfiguration, creates critical identity-based attack paths in cloud environments.

news.title: Lessons for organizations from the Verizon 2026 Data Breach Investigations Report
news.summary:
This is my favourite time of the year, not just because spring is here and the promise of summer is on the way. But also, because one of my must reads each year gets published. There are a few must read reports that I have on my reading list for each year and the Verizon Data Breach Investigations Report is on top of that list. The latest Verizon 2026 Data Breach Investigations Report (DBIR) once … More →

The post Lessons for organizations from the Verizon 2026 Data Breach Investigations Report appeared first on Help Net Security.

news.title: TrapDoor Supply Chain Attack Spreads Credential-Stealing Malware via npm, PyPI, and CratesIO
news.summary: A coordinated supply chain attack campaign called TrapDoor has distributed credential-stealing malware across npm, PyPI, and Crates.io via over 34 malicious packages spanning 384 versions. The campaign began on May 22, 2026, with packages published in waves targeting developers across multiple package ecosystems.

news.title: npm Adds 2FA-Gated Publishing and Package Install Controls Against Supply Chain Attacks
news.summary: GitHub has introduced staged publishing for npm, requiring maintainers to complete a 2FA challenge before packages become publicly available. The feature adds human-gated approval controls to npm publishing workflows as a supply chain security measure.

news.title: Working with Claims Mapping Policies in Microsoft Entra ID
news.summary: The article covers how to use Claims Mapping Policies in Microsoft Entra ID to customize tokens for OAuth, OpenID Connect, and SAML applications. It explains how to add user attributes such as first and last name to tokens and assign these policies to Service Principals.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a significant credential leak. Lawmakers from both chambers of Congress are demanding answers while CISA works to contain the breach and invalidate the exposed credentials.

news.title: Security hardening to the autoupgrade process for Microsoft Entra Connect and Microsoft Entra Connect Health - Microsoft Entra ID
news.summary: Microsoft has implemented security hardening changes to the autoupgrade process for Microsoft Entra Connect and Microsoft Entra Connect Health. The update affects how these identity synchronization and monitoring tools automatically update themselves, requiring IAM administrators to review and potentially adjust their deployment configurations.

news.title: Microsoft recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Workforce Identity Security Platforms
news.summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Workforce Identity Security Platforms, Q2 2026. The company received the highest scores in both the current offering and strategy categories in this analyst evaluation of identity security platforms.

news.title: Identity security for AI agents: The proliferation challenge
news.summary: The article addresses the security challenges identity teams face when managing AI agents, which differ fundamentally from human identities and traditional non-human identities (NHIs). The piece focuses on how nondeterministic AI agent behavior creates new requirements for identity governance and access management frameworks.

news.title: Most data breaches start with a stolen password. Here’s how to fix that
news.summary: The article addresses the widespread problem of poor password hygiene in organizations, including credential reuse, sharing, and insecure storage practices. It appears to be a general advisory or opinion piece on password security practices rather than reporting on a specific event, product, or policy change.

news.title: GCC firms rethink cyber defences as AI phishing surges
news.summary: Security leaders in Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) firms are accelerating investments in identity security and rethinking trust models in response to AI-powered phishing campaigns expanding across platforms including Teams, calendars, and AI tools. The shift reflects growing adoption of zero trust frameworks as attack vectors move beyond traditional email-based phishing.

news.title: OktaがClaude Compliance APIと統合、Claude環境内のアイデンティティ保護を強化
news.summary: Okta has integrated its Identity Security Posture Management (ISPM) with Anthropic's Claude Compliance API to strengthen identity protection within Claude environments. The integration extends Okta's ISPM capabilities to monitor and manage identity security posture specifically for Claude AI deployments.

news.title: Microsoft Warns of Two Actively Exploited Defender Vulnerabilities
news.summary: Microsoft has disclosed two actively exploited vulnerabilities in Microsoft Defender, including a privilege escalation flaw (CVE-2026-41091, CVSS 7.8) that allows attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges via improper link resolution. A denial-of-service flaw in Defender is also under active exploitation in the wild.

news.title: Why Okta (OKTA) Sees AI Agents as an Identity-Security Opportunity
news.summary: Okta is positioning AI agents as an identity security opportunity, according to analysis framing the company as a turnaround investment. The article discusses Okta's stock performance and strategic direction around AI agent identity management.

news.title: WSO2 Accelerates Agentic Enterprise Adoption with New Agent Identity, Forward Deployed Engineers, and Expanded Delivery Partner Ecosystem
news.summary: WSO2 has expanded its Agent Fabric platform with new agent identity capabilities designed for AI-native enterprise environments. The update includes forward deployed engineering services and an expanded delivery partner ecosystem to support secure AI agent identity management across cloud systems.

news.title: Semperis Takes Center Stage at Infosec Europe 2026
news.summary: Semperis, an identity-driven cyber resilience vendor, announced its participation at Infosec Europe 2026. The highlight of their presence is a panel discussion featuring several cybersecurity professionals on the topic of "Midnight in the War Room."

news.title: Protecting collaboration with behavior-based access controls
news.summary: The article discusses behavior-based access controls as a security approach for protecting modern collaboration sessions. It argues that traditional security tools are insufficient and positions behavior-based access controls as an alternative or complement to conventional access management methods.

news.title: Implement SAML SSO Authentication in Laravel Filament with Socialite
news.summary: This article provides a technical tutorial on implementing SAML SSO authentication in Laravel Filament using the Socialite library and SAML2 provider. The guide covers authentication flow components including metadata endpoints, redirects, and callbacks for enterprise application development.

news.title: Agent AI is Coming. Are You Ready?
news.summary: Orchid Security released its "Identity Gap: Snapshot 2026" report on May 19th, 2026, examining unmanaged identity risks in enterprise environments. The report found that "identity dark matter" — unseen and unmanaged identity elements — now accounts for 57% of enterprise identity landscape, with the trend coinciding with increased enterprise adoption of Agent AI.

news.title: CISA Exposes Secrets, Credentials in 'Private' Repo
news.summary: CISA inadvertently exposed secrets and credentials in a GitHub repository that was publicly accessible since November 2025. The repository, ironically named "Private-CISA," contained sensitive credentials that were visible to anyone accessing the public repo.

news.title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments
news.summary: Vulnerabilities dubbed "Claw Chain" were discovered in the OpenClaw AI agent framework, allowing attackers to steal credentials, escalate privileges, and maintain persistence. The flaws have since been patched in what is described as a rapidly growing AI agent deployment environment.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed credentials to highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems via a public GitHub repository. The leaked files included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, constituting a significant privileged credential exposure incident.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. The charges stem from SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in the theft of tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on token theft to silently compromise user authentication credentials at scale.
